definition: A clear step-by-step set of rules to solve a problem or task.
example: Route apps use an algorithm to pick the fastest road.
usageNote: Algorithms appear in coding, cooking, and even morning routines — any ordered steps.

definition: A nutrient from grains, fruit, and starch that gives quick energy.
example: Oats contain fiber-rich carbohydrates.
usageNote: Complex carbs (whole grains) digest slower than simple sugars.

definition: A race where teammates take turns; also an electrical switch.
example: She anchored the four-by-one-hundred relay.
usageNote: In track, dropping the baton disqualifies the team — precision matters.

definition: Living things and their environment working as one system.
example: Removing wolves changed the whole forest ecosystem.
usageNote: Keystone species hold ecosystems together; losing one can collapse the web.

definition: Something that speeds a reaction without being used up.
example: Enzymes act as catalysts inside cells.
usageNote: Metaphorically, a person or event can be a catalyst for social change.

definition: A testable prediction based on observation before an experiment.
example: Our hypothesis was that warmer soil would sprout seeds faster.
usageNote: If experiments reject a hypothesis, scientists revise — failure is data.

definition: How much data a network can carry at once; also mental capacity for tasks.
example: Video calls need more bandwidth than text messages.
usageNote: Saying 'I don't have bandwidth' means you're overloaded, not talking about Wi-Fi only.

definition: Stamina to keep performing despite fatigue.
example: Rowers build endurance with long steady sessions.
usageNote: Endurance training differs from sprint power — both matter in team sports.

definition: A close relationship where two species benefit from living together.
example: Clownfish and sea anemones show symbiosis on reefs.
usageNote: Not all symbiosis is friendly — parasites also live in close contact.

definition: To soak food in seasoned liquid before cooking.
example: Marinate the chicken overnight for deeper flavor.
usageNote: Acidic marinades can 'cook' fish ceviche without heat.

definition: To locate and fix errors in software.
example: We debugged the loop that skipped every other row.
usageNote: Rubber-duck debugging — explaining code aloud — often reveals the bug.

definition: Speed with direction; how fast and which way something moves.
example: The pitcher's velocity peaked in the late innings.
usageNote: Velocity is a vector; speed alone has no direction.
